person.name: Adarsh Uday
person.title: Engineer across physical systems, supply chains, and digital products
intro: I work where physical engineering, operations thinking, and digital execution meet.
From IIT Madras to Georgia Tech, the throughline is the same: build systems that are clear, useful, and hard to ignore.
person.currentMindset: Clarity over noise. Rigor without flatness.
currentFocus: M.S. in Supply Chain Engineering at Georgia Tech.
Expanding engineering, analytics, and product work into sharper case studies.
Interested in systems that connect physical operations, decision models, and usable interfaces.

highlightSections.title: Georgia Tech
highlightSections.summary: Graduate work in demand forecasting, network optimization, stochastic inventory, and facility systems design.

highlightSections.title: IIT Madras
highlightSections.summary: Mechanical design, manufacturing systems, embedded research, campus product building, and leadership across the B.Tech years.

highlightSections.title: Achievements and Schooling
highlightSections.summary: Scholastic milestones, exam performance, and the academic foundations that shaped the work.

masters.headline: Georgia Tech - M.S. in Supply Chain Engineering
summary: The Georgia Tech track is centered on quantitative decision-making, systems design, and operations strategy.
The projects here move from dining demand and network monitoring to stochastic inventory and facility design.
focusAreas: Facility systems design and storage strategy
masters.buildNote: The strongest themes right now are demand, networks, uncertainty, and facility systems.

bachelors.headline: IIT Madras - B.Tech in Mechanical Engineering
summary: The IIT Madras side of the archive spans mechanism design, manufacturing systems, undergraduate research, entrepreneurship, campus product building, and embedded prototyping.
It spans mechanisms, manufacturing systems, prosthetics, analytics, and campus-built software.
focusAreas: Embedded systems, biomechanics, and campus software products
bachelors.buildNote: The academic work sits alongside leadership, extracurricular life, and early product experiments from the IIT Madras years.

sections.title: How I work
body: I like work that starts with a messy system and ends with a structure that is clear enough to reason about, build, and improve.
That is true whether the problem is a mechanical linkage, a dining-hall forecast, a supply chain model, or a campus software product.

sections.title: What ties the projects together
body: Across domains, the common thread is systems thinking: constraints, flow, decisions, interfaces, and implementation tradeoffs.
I care about turning abstract analysis into something that can be demonstrated, tested, or deployed.

sections.title: Why this site exists
body: I want the work to live at full resolution instead of being flattened into resume bullets.
Over time each project page should show the decisions, tradeoffs, artifacts, and outcomes behind the line items.

leadership.title: Academic Mentor
leadership.subtitle: Avanti Fellows, Pondicherry Chapter
leadership.summary: Supported underprivileged students through long-term academic mentoring, doubt clearing, and motivational support in a PAN-India education initiative.
bullets: Mentored 50-plus underprivileged students in collaboration with 30 mentors.
Personally mentored one student for two years through the IIT admissions journey.
Ran 10-plus focused doubt-solving and motivation sessions for a small student cohort.

leadership.title: Finance and Operations Manager
leadership.subtitle: Entrepreneurship Cell, IIT Madras
leadership.summary: Ran operations and budgeting work for startup-facing events while coordinating fundraising and ecosystem outreach.
bullets: Raised upwards of INR 250 million for more than 250 startups through a nationwide fundraiser competition.
Helped execute six startup meetups that connected 300-plus startups with VCs and enablers.
Led recruitment and training for 12 associate managers on budgeting and event coordination.

extracurriculars.title: Drama Club Contingent Member
extracurriculars.subtitle: Sangam, IIT Madras
extracurriculars.summary: Worked as both performer and production collaborator across institute theatre, festival competition, and inter-IIT cultural stages.
bullets: Performed as lead actor at Inter-IIT Culturals in 2023 and 2024.
Won second place at Saarang in an original mystery stage production.
Headed production work for an original comedy play that won first place at two major college festivals.

extracurriculars.title: Fashion Club Contingent Member
extracurriculars.subtitle: Sangam, IIT Madras
extracurriculars.summary: Part of the institute fashion contingent, contributing to team ramp walks and festival appearances across campuses.
bullets: Selected into the 20-member fashion contingent at the institute.
Participated in annual festivals across the country through coordinated team performances.
